title: Recover and display members data after deployment issues

requirements.summary: Add data recovery status banner and reload functionality to AdminPanel to help users recover member data after deployment issues
acceptanceCriteria: When members are present, the banner shows the correct member count.
When the members list is empty, a warning message is displayed with a Reload button.
Clicking the Reload button triggers a fresh fetch of members from the backend.
The UI reflects the updated member list after reloading.

file_operations.description: Add a data recovery status banner at the top of the members section that displays the count of loaded members when present. When the members list is empty, show a prominent warning message ('No members found — data may not have loaded. Please use the Reload button or contact support.') with a clearly labeled 'Reload Members' button. Wire the button to trigger queryClient.invalidateQueries for the listMembersByName query key to force a fresh fetch from the backend. Ensure the banner is visible and positioned above the existing members list/table section. Use the authorization component's authentication state to ensure users are logged in before displaying member data.

Review the useListMembersByName query hook to ensure it properly exposes loading, error, and data states that AdminPanel.tsx can consume for the recovery banner. Verify that the query key is correctly named so that invalidateQueries works as expected. No changes needed if the hook already returns standard React Query states (isLoading, isError, data, refetch).
